candidates.quote: There sits the only king I mean to bow my knee to, m’lords,” he thundered. “The King in the North!
candidates.note: Acclaimed King in the North by the Greatjon and the northern/river lords (Stark side).

candidates.quote: All hail His Grace, Joffrey of the Houses Baratheon and Lannister, the First of his Name, King of the Andals and the Rhoynar and the First Men, Lord of the Seven Kingdoms and Protector of the Realm
candidates.note: Crowned king on the Iron Throne (Lannister/crown side).

candidates.quote: I am the rightful king, and your son no less a traitor than my brother here.
candidates.note: Self-proclaimed king by right of birth (Stannis's own side, Dragonstone).

candidates.quote: King Renly is the crowned and anointed lord of all the Seven Kingdoms, my lady,
candidates.note: Self-proclaimed king with the great southron host at Bitterbridge (Renly's own side).

candidates.quote: I am the Greyjoy, Lord Reaper of Pyke, King of Salt and Rock, Son of the Sea Wind, and no man gives me a crown.
candidates.note: Declares himself King of the Iron Islands (ironborn/Greyjoy side).

candidates.quote: In the dawn light, the army of Lord Tywin Lannister unfolded like an iron rose, thorns gleaming.
candidates.note: Commands the westerlands host for the crown (Lannister side); not a claimant king but the war's principal field commander on that side.

candidates.quote: Rainbow lights danced once more about the High Septon’s crown as he solemnly declared Joffrey of the Houses Baratheon and Lannister and Margaery of House Tyrell to be one flesh, one heart, one soul.
candidates.note: Performs the wedding rite.

candidates.quote: “With this kiss I pledge my love!” Joffrey declared in ringing tones.
candidates.note: Groom at his own wedding feast.

candidates.quote: When Margaery echoed the words he pulled her close and kissed her long and deep.
candidates.note: Bride at the wedding.

candidates.quote: Tyrion listened with half a ear, as he sampled sweetcorn fritters and hot oatbread baked with bits of date, apple, and orange, and gnawed on the rib of a wild boar.
candidates.note: Guest at the feast; later falsely accused of poisoning Joffrey (that accusation/death has its own node, not this wedding).

candidates.quote: “My lady.” Tyrion offered Sansa his arm. She took it dutifully, but he could feel her stiffness as they walked up the aisle together.
candidates.note: Guest, escorted by her husband Tyrion.

candidates.quote: Cersei embraced Margaery and kissed her cheeks.
candidates.note: Guest of honor, queen mother.

candidates.quote: Lord Tywin did the same, and then Lancel and Ser Kevan.
candidates.note: Guest of honor; "the same" refers to embracing/kissing Margaery (previous clause).

candidates.quote: Lord Tywin almost always chose to command the reserve; he would take the high ground and watch the battle unfold below him, committing his forces when and where they were needed most.
candidates.note: Overall Lannister commander, holds the reserve.

candidates.quote: His uncle would lead the center.
candidates.note: Commands the Lannister center (archers, pikemen, heavy horse).

candidates.quote: Ser Addam Marbrand had the command.
candidates.note: Commands the Lannister right wing (cavalry).

candidates.quote: Ser Gregor waved his huge sword and bellowed a command, and a thousand other voices screamed back at him.
candidates.note: Commands the Lannister vanguard (including Tyrion's clansmen).

candidates.quote: The larger part of the northern host, pikes and archers and great masses of men-at-arms on foot, remained upon the east bank under the command of Roose Bolton.
candidates.note: Commands the northern foot host (Stark side) in the battle.

candidates.quote: A man-at-arms thrust at his chest and his axe lashed out, knocking the spear aside.
candidates.note: Fights in the vanguard.

candidates.quote: Bronn was surrounded by three foes, but he lopped the head off the first spear that came at him, and raked his blade across a second man’s face on his backslash.
candidates.note: Fights beside Tyrion in the vanguard.

candidates.quote: Lord Hornwood is dead, and I fear Roose Bolton has escaped us.
candidates.note: Killed fighting on the Stark side.

candidates.quote: Robert with his warhammer and his great antlered helm, the Targaryen prince armored all in black.
candidates.note: Rebel leader; personally kills Rhaegar at the Trident (rebel side).

candidates.quote: “I came down on Storm’s End to lift the siege,” Ned told them, “and the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne dipped their banners, and all their knights bent the knee to pledge us fealty.
candidates.note: Rebel commander who lifts the siege of Storm's End (rebel side).

candidates.quote: When the Mad King Aerys II Targaryen had demanded their heads, the Lord of the Eyrie had raised his moon-and-falcon banners in revolt rather than give up those he had pledged to protect.
candidates.note: Instigator/commander who first raises the rebellion (rebel side, Vale).

candidates.quote: “Your brother Rhaegar brought as many men to the Trident,” Ser Jorah admitted,
candidates.note: Commands the loyalist host at the Trident (crown/loyalist side).

candidates.quote: So His Grace commanded his alchemists to place caches of wildfire all over King’s Landing.
candidates.note: King who orders the defense/destruction of King's Landing (crown/loyalist side); did not personally fight this act, hence COMMANDS_IN rather than AGENT_IN.

candidates.quote: Wounded by arrow, spear, and sword at the Battle of the Trident whilst fighting beside his Sworn Brothers and Rhaegar Prince of Dragonstone.
candidates.note: Kingsguard combatant, loyalist side.

candidates.quote: As his men died around him, Littlefinger slid Ned’s dagger from its sheath and shoved it up under his chin.
candidates.note: Overpowered and captured at knifepoint by Littlefinger, ending the chapter.

candidates.quote: As his men died around him, Littlefinger slid Ned’s dagger from its sheath and shoved it up under his chin. His smile was apologetic. “I did warn you not to trust me, you know.
candidates.note: Personally betrays and physically subdues Ned, completing the arrest (matches existing sub-beat littlefinger-betrays-ned).

candidates.quote: “You condemn yourself with your own mouth, Lord Stark,” said Cersei Lannister. “Ser Barristan, seize this traitor.
candidates.note: Orders Ned's seizure (crown/Lannister side; matches existing sub-beat cersei-orders-ned-s-arrest).

candidates.quote: “Kill him!” the boy king screamed down from the Iron Throne. “Kill all of them, I command it!
candidates.note: Orders Ned and his men killed from the Iron Throne.

candidates.quote: “Men of the Watch!” Janos Slynt shouted, donning his helm. A hundred gold cloaks leveled their spears and closed.
candidates.note: Commands the City Watch that turns on Ned's household guard (matches existing sub-beat gold-cloaks-betray-ned).

candidates.quote: Janos Slynt himself slashed open Varly’s throat.
candidates.note: Personally kills Varly during the melee, distinct from his COMMANDS_IN role over the Watch.

candidates.quote: Sandor Clegane’s first cut took off Cayn’s sword hand at the wrist; his second drove him to his knees and opened him from shoulder to breastbone.
candidates.note: Kills Cayn, one of Ned's household guard, during the arrest.

candidates.quote: Sandor Clegane’s first cut took off Cayn’s sword hand at the wrist; his second drove him to his knees and opened him from shoulder to breastbone.
candidates.note: Killed by Sandor Clegane during the melee.

candidates.quote: With a single sharp thrust, the nearest gold cloak drove his spear into Tomard’s back.
candidates.note: Killed by a gold cloak's spear (agent unnamed in text).

candidates.quote: Janos Slynt himself slashed open Varly’s throat.
candidates.note: Killed by Janos Slynt.

candidates.quote: Daenerys Targaryen wed Khal Drogo with fear and barbaric splendor in a field beyond the walls of Pentos, for the Dothraki believed that all things of importance in a man’s life must be done beneath the open sky.
candidates.note: Bride.

candidates.quote: there Dany was seated beside Khal Drogo, above the seething sea of Dothraki.
candidates.note: Groom.

candidates.quote: Viserys was seated just below her, splendid in a new black wool tunic with a scarlet dragon on the chest.
candidates.note: Guest of honor, bride's brother.

candidates.quote: Illyrio and Ser Jorah sat beside him.
candidates.note: Guest, host who arranged the match.

candidates.quote: Illyrio and Ser Jorah sat beside him.
candidates.note: Guest of honor, place near the khal's bloodriders.

candidates.quote: The king himself marched northwest, to the Gods Eye and the newly completed Harrenhal,
candidates.note: Leads the central Targaryen thrust against Harrenhal in person.

candidates.quote: With them went Queen Visenya and Vhagar.
candidates.note: Commands the fleet thrust toward Gulltown/the Vale with Daemon Velaryon.

candidates.quote: Queen Rhaenys accompanied him, astride Meraxes of the golden eyes and silver scales.
candidates.note: Commands the southern thrust toward Storm's End alongside Orys Baratheon.

candidates.quote: The greater part of his host crossed the Blackwater Rush, making south for Storm’s End under the command of Orys Baratheon.
candidates.note: Field commander of the host marching on Storm's End.

candidates.quote: Once inside, he sent every man of his to the parapets, armed with spears and bows and crossbows, promising lands and riches to whichever of them could bring the dragon down.
candidates.note: Opposing king, commands the defense of Harrenhal.

candidates.quote: As Argilac the Arrogant gathered his swords at Storm's End, pirates from the Stepstones descended on the shores of Cape Wrath to take advantage of their absence,
candidates.note: Opposing king, mobilizes to defend Storm's End against Orys Baratheon.

candidates.quote: King Mern had brought half again as many men to the battle as King Loren, and so demanded the honor of commanding the center.
candidates.note: Opposing king, commands the allied westerlands/Reach center at the Field of Fire; dies there.

candidates.quote: King Loren and his knights would form the right, Lord Oakheart the left.
candidates.note: Opposing king, commands the westerlands right wing at the Field of Fire; survives and later bends the knee.

candidates.quote: Torrhen Stark, King in the North, had crossed the Neck and entered the riverlands, leading an army of savage northmen thirty thousand strong.
candidates.note: Opposing king, leads the northern host to the Trident before submitting without battle (the King Who Knelt).

candidates.quote: Prince Daemon would lead the assault on Harrenhal, riding Caraxes.
candidates.note: Leads the black assault on Harrenhal in person.

candidates.quote: its elderly castellan Ser Simon Strong (uncle to the late Lord Lyonel, greatuncle to Lord Larys) was quick to strike his banners when Caraxes lighted atop Kingspyre Tower.
candidates.note: De facto green commander/castellan of Harrenhal in Larys Strong's absence; surrenders the castle.

candidates.quote: His Grace commanded “all leal lords” to take the field and disperse the Faith's ragged hordes by force.
candidates.note: Crown side; orders the lords of the realm to put down the Faith Militant.

candidates.quote: At the behest of the Dowager Queen, they took command of the thousands of Targaryen loyalists in the city and surrounded the Hill of Rhaenys.
candidates.note: Crown side; directs the Kingsguard and loyalists against the Warrior's Sons after Maegor's Trial of Seven.

candidates.quote: seven hundred knights in silvered steel led by their grand captain, Ser Damon Morrigen, called Damon the Devout.
candidates.note: Faith Militant side; grand captain of the Warrior's Sons.

candidates.quote: There nine thousand Poor Fellows under Wat the Hewer found themselves caught between six lordly hosts as they attempted to cross the Mander.
candidates.note: Faith Militant side; leads the Poor Fellows host destroyed at Stonebridge/Bitterbridge.

candidates.quote: By then Ser Horys Hill had reached the Great Fork of the Blackwater with an even larger host; close on thirteen thousand Poor Fellows,
candidates.note: Faith Militant side; leads the larger Poor Fellows host at the Great Fork against King Maegor.

candidates.quote: Styr had lost all hope of taking Castle Black unawares when Jon escaped him, yet even so, he need not have warned of his approach so bluntly.
candidates.note: Wildling/Thenn side; joint commander of the raiding force sent to take Castle Black from the south.

candidates.quote: Jarl was with the Magnar; Mance had given them the joint command.
candidates.note: Wildling side; joint commander with Styr of the force that scales the Wall.

candidates.quote: I suppose I’m as much a commander as we have now.
candidates.note: Night's Watch side; de facto commander of Castle Black's defense.

candidates.quote: Jon got off three arrows for every bolt Deaf Dick discharged,
candidates.note: Fights as an archer in the defense.

candidates.quote: He found Ygritte sprawled across a patch of old snow beneath the Lord Commander’s Tower, with an arrow between her breasts.
candidates.note: Killed by an arrow in the fighting; shooter not named in text (honest-whodunit, no AGENT_IN proposed).

candidates.quote: all the queer wild folk Mance had gathered to break the Wall.
candidates.note: Wildling king; commands the main host besieging the gate at Castle Black.

candidates.quote: Donal Noye turned toward the two great trebuchets that Bowen Marsh had restored to working order. “Give me light!” he roared.
candidates.note: Night's Watch side; commands the defense until he goes down to hold the tunnel gate.

candidates.quote: “Load the trebuchet with caltrops,” Jon said. “Owen, Kegs, angle the catapults toward the center.
candidates.note: Takes command of the defense atop the Wall after Noye goes down into the tunnel.

candidates.quote: Noye’s sword was sunk deep in the giant’s throat, halfway to the hilt.
candidates.note: Kills Mag the Mighty in the gate tunnel.

candidates.quote: “The giant crushed his spine. I don’t know who died first.”
candidates.note: Dies in the tunnel fighting Mag the Mighty.

candidates.quote: “It was Mag the Mighty. The king of the giants.”
candidates.note: Killed by Donal Noye's sword in the tunnel (mutual kill with Noye).

candidates.quote: “Open the south gate,” she commanded, even as the north gate shook to the impact of the ram.
candidates.note: Ironborn side; commands the defense and breakout from Deepwood Motte.

candidates.quote: Stannis had smashed Mance Rayder’s wildlings at the Wall and cleaned Asha and her ironborn out of Deepwood Motte;
candidates.note: Commands the northern/Night's Watch-allied force that takes Deepwood Motte back.

candidates.quote: Tris had gained the saddle, and his mount was rearing and wheeling as he laid about with his sword.
candidates.note: Ironborn combatant.

candidates.quote: Qarl stood over him, with his longsword dripping and moonlight shining in his eyes.
candidates.note: Ironborn combatant, fights beside Asha.

candidates.quote: A heartbeat later another bush killed Quenton, driving an axe into the base of his skull.
candidates.note: Killed in the ambush in the wolfswood after fleeing Deepwood Motte.

candidates.quote: Ser Tyland Lannister, recently returned from Myr, was made Hand of the King,
candidates.note: Hand of the King, handles day-to-day rule during the regency; existing MEMBER_OF-style edges don't capture his active governing role.

candidates.quote: whilst Lord Leowyn Corbray was named Protector of the Realm.
candidates.note: Protector of the Realm, responsible for defense during the regency.

candidates.quote: The most influential of those was plainly the Sea Snake, whose wealth, experience, and alliances made him the first amongst equals.
candidates.note: First among equals of the regents until his death in 132 AC; overlaps with the existing MEMBER_OF edge but reifies his active governing influence.

candidates.quote: Yet it was Grand Maester Munkun who put an end to the debate when he said, “My lords, it makes no matter. They are both girls.
candidates.note: Grand Maester and regent throughout; actively shapes council decisions (succession debate).

candidates.quote: Lord Corlys's seat upon the council of regents had already been offered to an older and more seasoned man: Unwin Peake,
candidates.note: Joins the regency council after Corlys's death; later becomes Hand.

flags: war-of-the-five-kings: Tywin Lannister's role in Robert's Rebellion (see roberts-rebellion) is nominally neutral until he sacks King's Landing for Robert at the very end — not proposed as COMMANDS_IN there, but flagging in case the orchestrator wants a note-only mention.
purple-wedding: no VICTIM_IN proposed for Joffrey per the harm-gate rule stated in the task brief (event.wedding is not a harm subtype); his death has its own node (death-of-joffrey-baratheon).

arrest-of-eddard-stark: Sansa Stark does NOT qualify for WITNESS_IN on this event — she is behind a closed, barred door and only hears the killing ('it was hard not to be terrified when the killing began... She heard it as she had never heard it before'), never sees it. This fails the text-anchor gate (contrast with the rule's own Sansa example at Ned's later execution, which is a different scene/node). No WITNESS_IN proposed.

aegons-conquest: Torrhen Stark (torrhen-stark) is proposed as COMMANDS_IN despite submitting without battle — he did lead an army of 30,000 to the Trident before kneeling, which the text treats as a real military mobilization, not merely diplomatic.

battle-beneath-the-wall: Stannis Baratheon's relief force is described in this node's own Origins prose (fleet under Salladhor Saan, Cotter Pyke's knights) but the actual clash/rescue is not shown on-page in the ASOS Jon chapters available (it's revealed later, secondhand); existing edge 'in:CAUSES:stannis-moves-to-the-wall' already models this as a separate downstream event, so no Stannis role edge proposed here — flagging in case a later chunk covering ADWD material finds on-page grounding.

regency-of-aegon-iii: all five PARTICIPATES_IN candidates overlap with existing MEMBER_OF-style edges on this node (per the packet's existing_edges list); proposed anyway per SHARED-RULES guidance that role edges may still be valid even when an equivalent typed edge exists, since PARTICIPATES_IN reifies active governing behavior rather than static membership. Two other named regents (Jeyne Arryn, Torrhen Manderly, Roland Westerling, Royce Caron, Manfryd Mooton) were left out to avoid an exhaustive roster — only the most load-bearing/active figures were proposed.

assault-on-harrenhal: Larys Strong himself (nominal Lord of Harrenhal) was NOT proposed for any role — he is in King's Landing during the assault and never acts; only his castellan Ser Simon Strong is grounded as the actual (surrendering) commander on-site.
